checks.finding: REQ-001 (requirements.md Goals) states a byte-level normalization rule: delete column-0 Second Approval lines (including terminator) before hashing, for any field value, LF and CRLF, task-stage only, with sh/ps1 required to be byte-identical and decision-identical. Fully observable/measurable by AC-001..AC-004/TEST-001..004.
The single Goal REQ-001 is traced by AC-001 through AC-005, each tagged (REQ-001) in requirements.md and each mapped to TEST-001..TEST-005 in acceptance-tests.md.

AC-001..AC-005 each specify externally checkable outcomes: exit code + workflow-state: ok text, nonzero exit + stage-provenance/task plan hash is stale diagnostic, byte-identical normalized forms, matching first diagnostic rule IDs, and SHA-256 equality of live vs. staged human-copy files.

requirements.md Non-goals explicitly excludes check-task-state content validation, the hook guard write-denial rule, spec/impl-stage normalization, the identity ledger, registry schema, other validators, and retroactive re-hashing of existing contracts.

Security Boundaries names the removed tamper-evidence class and compensating controls (hook guard denial, check-task-state Done-time validation); Roles and Permissions states the human-only write constraint and the mandated human-copy/SHA-256-MANIFEST staging path required by the hook guard's R-10 protected-suffix rule; Assumptions states the pwsh 7.x dependency.

All three Risks entries map to concrete validation surfaces: over-broad masking -> AC-002/TEST-002 negative controls including the column-0-anchoring sub-case; sh/ps1 divergence -> AC-003/AC-004 byte-identical normal-form assertions; botched live apply -> AC-005/TEST-005 human-copy MANIFEST plus gate-recorded live==staged SHA-256 evidence.
